Short description: Test whether fine-tuning on past open experiments changes the human role from first-pass prediction to calibration and scope setting.
Empirical unit of analysis: Model predictions over 210 open social-science experiments
Candidate roles tested: Calibration architect; counterfactual and transportability judge
Key typology question: What remains for humans once behavior-prediction quality improves materially after fine-tuning?
Observable artifacts / traces: Training/eval splits, distributional error metrics, fairness metrics, screening decisions
Key outcomes observed: Prediction alignment, generalization to new studies/conditions, bias reduction
What would weaken / falsify the role cla: Fine-tuned models generalize so well that human calibration rarely changes use decisions
Main confounds / risks of misreading: Benchmark overfitting can overstate real screening value
Why this case has high leverage: It pressures the most plausible transformed role under predictive AI: calibration.
collector_notes: Generated from workbook and workbook-linked sources.
